scope: Regionally node-positive MIBC (cN1–N3, cM0) — perioperative systemic therapy before possible cystectomy

pathway.title: Typical curative pathway for cN+ M0 MIBC

steps.label: Diagnosis & staging
steps.detail: TURBT confirms muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma. CT/MRI + PET help stage nodes. cN1 = clinically involved regional pelvic node(s); cM0 = no distant metastases.

steps.label: Cisplatin eligibility
steps.detail: If fit for cisplatin: GC/ddMVAC, NIAGARA (durva+GC), or EV-304-type perioperative EV+pembro (if available). If unfit: KEYNOTE-905 / EV-ECLIPSE pathways.

steps.label: Systemic therapy first
steps.detail: Neoadjuvant or perioperative chemotherapy ± immunotherapy. Goal: shrink primary tumor and clear regional nodes (achieve pN0).

steps.label: Restaging
steps.detail: Repeat imaging (CT/PET) after systemic therapy. Response in nodes guides surgery timing and prognosis.

steps.label: Curative local treatment
steps.detail: Radical cystectomy + pelvic lymph node dissection if operable. Radiation in selected unfit/refusing patients — not primary focus of this site.

steps.label: Surgical pathology
steps.detail: ypT and ypN stage after systemic therapy. pN0 (node-negative at surgery) is a strong surrogate for cure in cN1 disease.

steps.label: Adjuvant if high risk remains
steps.detail: If pathology shows pT3/pT4 or persistent pN+: adjuvant nivolumab (CheckMate 274) or pembrolizumab (AMBASSADOR) may be considered.

pathway.key_fork: Your profile sits at: systemic therapy first → restaging → cystectomy, with strong interest in whether EV+pembro beats standard cisplatin NAC in cN1 M0.

glossary.definition: Muscle-invasive bladder cancer — tumor invades the detrusor muscle (≥ cT2). This site covers MIBC with regional node involvement only.
Clinical stage: one regional pelvic lymph node involved on imaging/exam (cN1), no distant metastases (cM0). Your example: PET-positive external iliac node.
Pathologic node-negative at surgery. After neoadjuvant therapy, ypN0 means nodes cleared — strongly associated with better survival in cN+ disease.
Pathologic complete response — no viable tumor in bladder (ypT0) at cystectomy. Useful surrogate; nodal response (ypN0) may matter more in cN1.
Systemic therapy given before and/or after definitive local treatment (usually cystectomy). Includes neoadjuvant and adjuvant phases.
Lymphovascular invasion seen on TURBT pathology. Associated with higher risk of nodal disease and worse prognosis.
Event-free or disease-free survival — time without recurrence, progression, or death (trial-specific definitions).
Compares event rates between arms. HR <1 favors experimental treatment. Subgroup HRs with wide CI crossing 1 are inconclusive.
Metastatic disease limited to lymph nodes without visceral metastases — as in EV-302 subgroup. Different from curative perioperative cN1 M0.
Adequate kidney function, hearing, performance status, and no neuropathy for cisplatin. Determines NIAGARA/EV-304 vs KEYNOTE-905 pathways.
How well a study matches this patient profile: A=direct perioperative cN+ match; B=partial; B−=nodal but wrong setting; C=wrong setting; D=indirect/retro.
How confident we are in the patient-count match: high=published or by design; moderate=estimated or retro; low=tiny subgroup or indirect.
